title: daphne cannabina var. bholua
intro: Daphne cannabina var. bholua, also known as Himalayan daphne, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Thymelaeaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly due to the presence of daphnetin, a lignan with potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing stress, alleviating respiratory issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Himalayan daphne is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and provide relief from various health conditions.
